name: success_is_error
reason: Success return value would be incorrectly classified as an error, causing all callers to treat successful operations as failures

name: quota_error_missed
reason: Quota exhaustion errors would go undetected, allowing callers to proceed as if allocation succeeded

name: mmap_always_returns_quota_error
code:
proof fn phi_3_mmap_always_returns_quota_error(os: OS, os_new: OS, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, va_range: VaRange4K, ret: SyscallReturnStruct)
    requires
        os.total_wf(),
        os.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len * 4 < usize::MAX,
        syscall_mmap_spec(os, os_new, thread_ptr, va_range, ret),
    ensures
        ret =~= SyscallReturnStruct::NoSwitchNew(RetValueType::ErrorNoQuota),
{
}
reason: mmap would unconditionally fail with ErrorNoQuota regardless of actual quota availability, making memory mapping impossible

name: else_not_error
reason: The catch-all error variant would be silently treated as success, hiding unexpected failure modes from callers

name: mmap_breaks_invariant
code:
proof fn phi_5_mmap_breaks_invariant(os: OS, os_new: OS, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, va_range: VaRange4K, ret: SyscallReturnStruct)
    requires
        os.total_wf(),
        os.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len * 4 < usize::MAX,
        syscall_mmap_spec(os, os_new, thread_ptr, va_range, ret),
    ensures
        !os_new.total_wf(),
{
}
reason: mmap would destroy the system's global well-formedness invariant, leaving the OS in an inconsistent state after any mapping call
